# Stripe Atlas Official Documentation (2026)

## Summary

Stripe Atlas is Stripe's formation service for Delaware C-Corps, LLCs, and C-Corp subsidiaries. Current price is $500 one-time (includes state filing fees, first year registered agent) plus $100/year thereafter. The service automatically handles: Certificate of Incorporation, EIN, equity issuance, 83(b) election filing, and introductions to Mercury and Brex for banking. 100,000+ founders have used Atlas. Legal documents co-created with Cooley LLP.

## Key quotations / statistics

- "Atlas costs 500 USD, which covers incorporation (plus state fees), and your first year of registered agent services. After that, we charge 100 USD annually to maintain your registered agent."
- "Stripe Atlas incorporates your company in Delaware, gets your company tax ID (EIN) from the IRS, issues founders equity, and files your 83(b) election."
- "Join 100,000+ founders who have used Stripe Atlas to incorporate in Delaware."
- "Startups in over 140 countries have chosen Atlas to start their business."
- "Atlas provides intros to Mercury, Carta and AngelList to provide faster, automatic onboarding."
- "All Atlas companies get $2,500 in Stripe product credits to use during the first year after incorporation, plus over $50,000 in discounts on tools."
- "Incorporation in Delaware usually takes 1-2 business days. Immediately after incorporation, Atlas automatically purchases shares on your behalf and issues them to you."
- "You can begin fundraising before the IRS assigns your EIN."
- Entity types supported: Delaware C-Corp, Delaware LLC, C-Corp subsidiary
- Registered agent renewal: $100/year
- C-Corp FMV caution: "Atlas forms C corporations with an industry standard fair market value (FMV) of 100 USD, which might not be accurate if you have substantially valuable IP."
- Processing time: "All Atlas applications include expedited 24-hour processing service at the state, for no extra fee."
- Non-US EIN: "Founders who provide a US Social Security number, US address and US phone are eligible for expedited processing; all other users will receive standard processing."

## Annotations for stinger-forge

- **guides/01-formation-platforms.md:** Use the $500 + $100/year structure as the canonical Atlas pricing. Note that the $50k+ in discounts is a major differentiator vs. Clerky.
- **guides/02-ein-workflow.md:** Atlas handles EIN filing automatically for most users; non-US founders need "standard processing" which is slower.
- **guides/05-founder-paperwork.md:** Atlas auto-files 83(b) election - this is a significant differentiator. Clerky and others provide templates/guidance but may not auto-file.
- **guides/06-attorney-triggers.md:** Atlas's standard FMV of $100 for IP is a flag - if the founder has contributed substantial IP, this may be inaccurate and an attorney is needed.
- Note the "December 31 caution" for tax year: "C corps and LLCs operating in Delaware before December 31 are subject to annual tax for that year. Annual tax isn't prorated. Waiting until January 1 to incorporate could save you a full year of Delaware annual tax."
